channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Here’s how Google is using LLMs for complex internal code migrations
item.description: Code migration is a critical process in maintaining software applications. It helps improve performance and resilience, keeps systems up to date, and removes stale or irrelevant code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google Introduces Vertex AI RAG Engine: Streamlining LLM Development
item.description: Google's new Vertex AI RAG Engine streamlines the process of retrieving relevant information from a knowledge base and feeding it to an LLM, empowering developers to build grounded, generative AI solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One reason the U.S. government is so spooked by TikTok: AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. The real reason the U.S. government wants to ban a ByteDance-owned TikTok TikTok could shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Toshiba launched two new portable hard drives, but I think they've got something really, really wrong
item.description: Toshiba’s new drives bring style and portability, but miss the mark with outdated USB Type-A reliance and no 6TB capacity option.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: SASE 2025: Impact grows despite adoption hurdles
item.description: Secure access service edge technologies will garner a lot of attention in 2025, according to a recent survey, but despite the many benefits SASE promises, network and security leaders face an equal number of challenges in successfully implementing the technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Doro's new senior-focused phones speak to you to confirm keyboard presses and can send emergency alerts to five people at once
item.description: Doro has revealed a new lineup of phones designed with seniors in mind, featuring simplified interfaces and hearing aid compatibility amidst other features.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Here are the Big Tech CEOs attending Trump's inauguration
item.description: The CEOs of several of the world’s biggest technology companies are planning to attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Schumer pushes for TikTok ban delay as deadline looms
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) on Thursday called for a looming TikTok ban to be delayed to give the social media company more time to find an American buyer. "We aren’t against TikTok. We want TikTok to keep going.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: If the iPhone 17 gets this rumored Face ID upgrade, I'll never call Apple unoriginal again
item.description: A new patent suggests Apple is working on fitting Face ID underneath the iPhone display – but there are still some challenges to overcome.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Surprise finding sheds light on what causes Huntington's disease, a devastating fatal brain disorder
item.description: Scientists are unraveling the mystery of what triggers Huntington’s disease, a devastating hereditary disorder that strikes in the prime of life.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Avery label maker confirms attack on its site, customer credit card info stolen
item.description: Avery was unknowingly hosting a credit card skimmer for half a year, exposing customer data to hackers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google ups Workspace price, makes Gemini AI features available for free
item.description: Google will no longer charge Workspace customers an additional fee for Gemini, expanding access to generative AI (genAI) features in apps such as Gmail, Docs, and Sheets. At the same time, the company is increasing the price of Workspace subscriptions by a small amount.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Soft robotic armband gives prosthetic hand users natural control
item.description: Researchers found in a study that users of a robotic prosthetic hand could successfully grasp and move two objects at once without dropping them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The only Android file manager you need
item.description: File management may not be the most exciting subject surrounding smartphone ownership — but if you use your device for productivity, my goodness, is it ever worth your while to chew over. Your phone, after all, is your on-the-go connection to the world.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Android file transfer: How to manage, move, and sync files
item.description: When you think about your smartphone, apps and interfaces are probably the first things that come to mind. Beneath all that surface-level stuff, though, our modern mobile devices are filled with files — folders upon folders of ’em! — just like the clunky ol’ computers we’ve relied upon for ages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 fintech predictions that will shape 2025
item.description: In a year defined by breakthroughs in artificial intelligence and blockchain technology, you might think that all this incredible innovation has made its way into the hands of the people who need it most. But you’d be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Siroya, Fast Company

item.title: It’s time for Apple to press pause on its AI news summaries
item.description: Apple should shut off its AI summaries of news alerts until it can prevent them from spreading fake news. Anecdotes of iPhone users seeing weirdly phrased summaries of texts and other content are common. But getting news headlines wrong is another matter.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Kazakhstan’s AI-Powered Prisons: Setting a New Standard for Safety and Efficiency
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Powerful Webb Telescope captures photos of one of the earliest supernova ever seen
item.description: The James Webb Space Telescope captured photos of one of the earliest supernovas ever seen using infrared technology, and creating a time lapse of the phenomena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will 'find a way' to preserve TikTok, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump will take action to preserve TikTok upon taking office, his incoming national security adviser said Wednesday, with a deadline looming for the app’s owner to sell or face a ban in the United States. Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: H-1B visa: South Bay executives must be re-charged with visa fraud, judges rule
item.description: Federal prosecutors claimed the two falsely stated in 85 visa applications that prospective H-1B holders would work on-site on internal projects at their San Jose computer chip business, PerfectVIPs.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: SEC slaps Elon Musk with a lawsuit. Here’s why
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Get ready to experience thrill of eVTOL racing
item.description: The Jetson One eVTOL showcases air racing potential, ushering in a new aviation era. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the race for eVTOL supremacy is just beginning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS
item.description: Steve Hernandez, Education’s CISO, and Brian Bordelon, Education’s deputy CIO, have moved to new agencies to take on similar roles. The post Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5
